title: महामृत्युञ्जय मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री महामृत्युञ्जय मन्त्रः ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
ॐ अस्य श्री महामृत्युञ्जय मन्त्रस्य वशिष्ठ ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री त्र्यम्बक (रुद्र) देवता।
ॐ बीजम्। जूँ शक्तिः। सः कीलकम्।
सर्वरोगनिवारणार्थे, आयुर्वृद्ध्यर्थे, मोक्षप्राप्त्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
चन्द्रार्कवह्निप्रतिमं त्रिनेत्रं
व्याघ्राजिनाम्बरधरं मुनिवृन्दवन्द्यम्।
जटाधरं पार्वतीवामभागं
महामृत्युञ्जयमहं प्रणमामि शम्भुम्॥

॥ मन्त्रः ॥
ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्।
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय माऽमृतात्॥

॥ अर्थः ॥
हम तीन नेत्रों वाले भगवान शिव (त्र्यम्बक) का यजन (पूजन) करते हैं,
जो सुगन्धित हैं अर्थात् सभी प्राणियों को आनन्द प्रदान करने वाले हैं,
जो पुष्टिवर्धन हैं अर्थात् सबका पोषण और शक्ति बढ़ाने वाले हैं।
जैसे ककड़ी अपनी बेल से पक कर स्वयं अलग हो जाती है,
वैसे ही हमें मृत्यु के बन्धन से मुक्त करें, अमृतत्व (मोक्ष) से कभी वंचित न करें।

॥ जप विधि ॥
रुद्राक्ष माला से 108 बार जप करें।
जल, दूध या बिल्वपत्र शिवलिंग पर चढ़ाते हुए जप करें।
महाशिवरात्रि, सोमवार या प्रदोष काल में जप अत्यन्त फलदायी है।

॥ इति श्री महामृत्युञ्जय मन्त्रः सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mahamrityunjaya Mantrah ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Om Asya Shri Mahamrityunjaya Mantrasya Vashishtha Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Tryambaka (Rudra) Devata.
Om Bijam. Jum Shaktih. Sah Kilakam.
Sarva-roga-nivaranarthe, Ayur-vriddhyarthe, Moksha-praptyarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Chandrarkaavahni-pratimam Trinetram
Vyaghrajina-ambara-dharam Muni-vrinda-vandyam.
Jatadharam Parvati-vamabhagam
Mahamrityunjayam-aham Pranamami Shambhum.

|| Mantrah ||
Om Tryambakam Yajamahe Sugandhim Pushti-vardhanam.
Urvarukam-iva Bandhanan Mrityor-mukshiya Ma-amritat.

|| Arthah ||
Ham Tina Netron Wale Bhagavan Shiva (Tryambaka) Ka Yajana (Pujana) Karte Hain,
Jo Sugandhit Hain Arthat Sabhi Praniyon Ko Ananda Pradana Karane Wale Hain,
Jo Pushtivardhana Hain Arthat Sabaka Poshana Aura Shakti Badhane Wale Hain.
Jaise Kakadi Apani Bela Se Paka Kara Svayam Alaga Ho Jati Hai,
Vaise Hi Hamein Mrityu Ke Bandhana Se Mukta Karen, Amritatva (Moksha) Se Kabhi Vanchit Na Karen.

|| Japa Vidhi ||
Rudraksha Mala Se 108 Bara Japa Karen.
Jala, Dudha Ya Bilvapatra Shivalinga Para Chadhate Hue Japa Karen.
Mahashivaratri, Somavara Ya Pradosha Kala Mein Japa Atyanta Phaladayi Hai.

|| Iti Shri Mahamrityunjaya Mantrah Sampurnah ||
hindiMeaning:
हम तीन नेत्रों वाले भगवान शिव की पूजा करते हैं जो सुगन्धित हैं (सभी प्राणियों को आनन्द देने वाले) और पुष्टिवर्धन हैं (सबका पोषण करने वाले)।

जिस प्रकार ककड़ी (उर्वारुक) पकने पर अपनी बेल (बन्धन) से स्वयं अलग हो जाती है, उसी प्रकार हमें मृत्यु के बन्धन से मुक्त करें, परन्तु अमृतत्व (मोक्ष) से वंचित न करें।

यह मन्त्र ऋग्वेद के सातवें मण्डल (7.59.12) से है। इसके ऋषि वशिष्ठ हैं। यह मन्त्र रोग निवारण, आयु वृद्धि, अकाल मृत्यु से रक्षा और मोक्ष प्राप्ति के लिए जपा जाता है। इसे 'मृत्यु को जीतने वाला मन्त्र' भी कहते हैं।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीमहामृत्युञ्जयमन्त्रस्य वशिष्ठ ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीत्र्यम्बकः (रुद्रः) देवता। ॐ बीजम्। जूँ शक्तिः। सः कीलकम्। सर्वरोगनिवारणार्थे आयुर्वृद्ध्यर्थे मोक्षप्राप्त्यर्थे महामृत्युञ्जयमन्त्रजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Most powerful Vedic mantra for conquering death and disease
Invokes longevity, good health, and freedom from chronic illness
Provides protection from accidents, calamities, and untimely death
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of death and bestows fearlessness
Purifies past karma and grants spiritual liberation (moksha)
Chanting 108 times with Rudraksha mala bestows immense blessings of Lord Shiva
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shiva, Mahamrityunjaya Mantra (महामृत्युञ्जय मन्त्र) stands as a sacred prayer. Attributed to Rigveda (7.59.12), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to grant longevity, good health, and freedom from chronic illness and provide protection from accidents, calamities, and untimely death. It is also said to remove fear of death and bestow fearlessness. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month. With 1 verses and a reading time of about 3 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Mahamrityunjaya Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Mahamrityunjaya Mantra in Sanskrit with viniyog, meaning and transliteration. Most powerful Vedic mantra for health, protection and longevity.
relatedSearches: Mahamrityunjaya Mantra meaning word by word
Mahamrityunjaya jaap vidhi rules at home
Maha Mrityunjay Mantra for serious illness recovery

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vedic source of the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mahamrityunjaya Mantra is preserved in the Rigveda, Mandala 7, hymn 59, verse 12, attributed to the seer Vasishtha as part of the Maitravaruni hymn group. The same verse also appears in the Yajurveda (Taittiriya Samhita 1.8.6.2 and Vajasaneyi Samhita 3.60), where it is used in Rudra worship. Because it appears in multiple Vedic shakhas and is central to Shaiva-Vedic ritual, it is considered one of the most universally accepted maha-mantras of Shiva alongside the Panchakshara Om Namah Shivaya.

seoFaqs.question: What does Tryambakam Yajamahe literally mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Tryambakam means the three-eyed one, an epithet of Rudra-Shiva referring to his eyes representing the sun, moon and fire. Yajamahe means we worship or we invoke through yajna. Sugandhim translates as fragrant, denoting the spreader of life-fragrance through all beings. Pushti-vardhanam means the increaser of nourishment and well-being. The simile urvarukam-iva bandhanan compares the soul to a ripe cucumber that effortlessly separates from its vine, a prayer that we be released from the bondage of mortality but not from amrita (immortality, liberation).

seoFaqs.question: How many times should the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional shastra prescribes 108 repetitions on a Rudraksha mala for daily protection, 1008 for a serious illness or transit affliction, and 125,000 (a laksha-jaap) for the complete purascharana that consecrates the mantra in the practitioner. For a critically ill person, families undertake an 11-day or 21-day jaap with a sankalpa for specific recovery, often performed by a priest at a Shiva temple with simultaneous bilva-archana and milk abhisheka of the lingam. Pradosh Kaal and Maha Shivaratri are considered the most fruit-bearing times.

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Mahamrityunjaya Mantra and Mritsanjeevani Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mahamrityunjaya Mantra is the seven-line Vedic mantra Om Tryambakam Yajamahe used universally for protection from death and disease. The Mritsanjeevani Mantra is the longer Tantric expansion taught by sage Shukracharya, beginning Om Hauṁ Joṁ Sah Bhuh Bhuvah Svah Tryambakam Yajamahe, which adds seed syllables (bija mantras) Hauṁ, Joṁ and Sah at the start and the Vyahritis at the end. The Mritsanjeevani is considered more potent for advanced practitioners who have received initiation, while the core Mahamrityunjaya is open to all householders without diksha.

seoFaqs.question: Who can chant the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra and are there restrictions?
seoFaqs.answer: The core Mahamrityunjaya Mantra is open to all without restriction of caste, gender or ashrama and is one of the most widely recited Vedic mantras in Hindu households. Children are often taught it as their first long mantra. Traditional acharya guidance recommends bathing before chanting, facing east or north, sitting on a clean asana of wool or kusha grass, using a Rudraksha mala, and avoiding chanting while reclining or eating. The Tantric Mritsanjeevani expansion with the bija syllables, however, requires guru-diksha before practice.

seoFaqs.question: What is the story of how the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra was given?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiva Purana narrates that sage Markandeya, born with a lifespan of only sixteen years, was a great Shiva devotee. When Yamaraja came on his sixteenth birthday to claim his life as he was embracing the Shiva lingam, Shiva himself emerged from the lingam, kicked Yama away and granted Markandeya immortality. The Mahamrityunjaya Mantra is the verse that Markandeya was chanting at that moment, and it is therefore considered the supreme victory-over-death mantra. This narrative is the basis for prescribing the mantra during life-threatening situations.
